shower.thought: The only time you see your face is when you're looking at a reflection or a photo—you've never actually seen what you look like, only evidence of it.
We spend our entire lives inside our own perspective, which means we're all the main character of a story everyone else is just a side character in.
Nostalgia is just grief for a time period that's still technically happening somewhere on Earth without you.
You can't remember most of your life, which means you're technically not the same person you were—you're just a stranger with your memories.
The moment you understand a joke is the exact moment it stops being funny, which means humor lives in confusion.
We call it "falling asleep" but we're actually letting go of consciousness on purpose every single night and trusting we'll come back.
Money is just a collective agreement to trade our time for future security, which means we're all on the same shared delusion.
Your phone knows you better than you know yourself, which is a weird thing to have accepted so casually.
The dead exist only in other people's memories, so forgetting someone might actually be worse than them never existing.
We fear death, but we also can't actually imagine being dead—we only imagine the process of dying, which might be completely different.
Every person you've ever met is the hero of their own story, which means you're also a side character in at least a thousand different narratives.
We keep diaries to remember our lives, but reading old diary entries makes us realize we've forgotten who we used to be.
The scariest part of getting older isn't the physical changes—it's realizing how many dead people you now understand better than living ones.
You've probably already seen the person you would have become if you'd made different choices—they just don't know they were almost you.
Rain is just clouds lowering their standards.
We spend money to buy things we think will make us happy, but we're really just trying to buy versions of ourselves that we think are better.
Your last conversation with someone might be your last one, and you'll never know it was the last conversation until it's too late.
The more evidence we find that the universe is random and meaningless, the more desperate we seem to find meaning in it.
We judge people based on their worst moments, but we judge ourselves based on our intentions, which is why everyone seems worse than they are and better than we are.
A song is just the same 12 notes arranged differently, yet somehow an infinite number of them still surprise us.

motivational.thought: The obstacle you're running from is usually the thing that will set you free. — Unknown
Success is not final and failure is not fatal. What matters is the courage to continue. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you haven't made yet. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of progress. Build your own thing anyway. — Unknown
The discomfort you feel right now is the price of admission to who you're becoming. — Unknown
You can't think your way into a new life. You have to live your way into it. — Unknown
Momentum builds quietly, then all at once you realize you've moved mountains. — Unknown
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is today. — Ancient Proverb
Regret weighs more than effort ever will. — Unknown
Your identity is not your circumstances. It's what you decide to do about them. — Unknown
Small daily choices stack into the life you didn't think was possible. — Unknown
The only person who needs to believe in you is the one in the mirror. — Unknown
Ease is a trap dressed up as comfort. — Unknown
You're not behind. You're not lost. You're exactly where your choices have taken you, and you can choose differently tomorrow. — Unknown
Patience is not about waiting. It's about continuing anyway while you wait. — Unknown
The work that matters most is the work nobody sees you do. — Unknown
Your capacity to endure is larger than your capacity to doubt. — Unknown
Fear is just a story you're telling yourself about a future that hasn't happened. — Unknown
What would you do if you were certain you couldn't fail? Start there. — Unknown
The person you want to be is already inside you. Everything else is just practice. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she'd be prettier if she smiled more.
Now she just smiles at other people.

My therapist asked what my biggest fear is.
I said it was him retiring before I got better.

I'm reading a book on the history of glue.
Can't put it down.

My dad said, "One day you'll understand why I left."
I'm 34 and still waiting for the explanation.

I went to a positive thinking seminar.
It was full of people who couldn't afford the refund.
The worst part about having insomnia is the nightmares you don't get to sleep through.

My friend said his job was killing him.
I said at least someone was getting something done.

I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.

My dad's in a band called "The Hindsight."
They're terrible, but at least he's seeing things clearly now.

My therapist said I'm making progress.
Then he sent me an invoice that proved I wasn't.

I asked the funeral director if my grandpa looked peaceful.
He said, "Better than when he was alive, honestly."

My motivational speaker was really inspiring.
Until he got deported and I realized he was just visa-versa about his life.

I told my boss I needed a mental health day.
He said, "Sure, take the rest of your life."

My wife asked if I still find her attractive.
I said, "I mean, I married you, didn't I?"
She said, "Exactly."

My kid asked why I had so many regrets.
I said, "Well, you're one of them."
He said, "Oh good, I thought it was something I did."

I started meditating to deal with my anxiety.
Now I'm just anxious in silence.

My doctor said I have a year to live.
My bank account said I have three months.
We're both right.

I told my friend he needed to stop making everything about him.
He said, "Yeah, you always do that."

My mom asked if I was seeing anyone.
I said only in the mirror, and even then I try not to.

I joined a support group for people afraid of commitment.
I haven't been to a second meeting.
